{"key_event":"Independence consolidation under O'Higgins","reasoning":"Civil society had some elite cabildos, press, and factional political mobilization, but participation was narrow, rural society remained hierarchical, and autonomous organization was limited. The new Chilean state defeated royalist resistance and built central institutions under O'Higgins, yet fiscal weakness, military dependence, and contested constitutional authority kept state capacity only moderate.","society_change":0.0,"society_power":3.5,"state_change":0.0,"state_power":5.0}
